entry.title: [Podfic] ship-to-ship combat
entry.summary:
Author: DevilWithABirdDress

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 231Chapters: 7/13Kudos: 55

Summary: Clark, in an attempt to make some spare cash, unintentionally stumbles into the world of superhero fanfiction, becomes a prolific writer for Gotham's OTP, and tries his best to fend off rival fans who want him to convert to superbat instead.Podfic of ship-to-ship combat by pomeloquat.

entry.title: The New Son of Krypton
entry.summary:
Author: Sally_Boomer

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,753Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 287

Summary: Superman whipped the condensation on the healing-vessel window with a shaky hand, the oppression on his chest only growing more overwhelming the more he cleared the view. Then he saw it. There, floating in the fluid, was a foetus. “Kelex.” Kal’s voice came broken and strangled, and a little hysterical, “Kelex, what is inside?” “A Kryptonian-human, Kal-El.” Replied the AI, “A descendant designed to better adapt to the planet chosen to board the House of El. A new type of Kryptonian for a new planet.” “A hybrid for…” Started Kal, only to stop mid-sentence, suddenly assaulted by a dreadful realisation. “Which human? Whose DNA did you take for this? From whom did you take the human sample?” “Your chosen mate, Kal-El. Batman.” Kelex answered without pause, as if the answer was obvious. ---- Superman, who has been secretly in love with his best friend for over a decade, accidentally baby traps Batman with a human-kryptonian child. Or Clark Kent and Bruce Wayne are Conner Wayne's biological parents, and they may or may not fall in love while raising their child together.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: The Weight of Never Leaving
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,767Chapters: 7/?Kudos: 10

Summary: Jason Todd has been dead for six hours and is already at the garden wall. Martha Wayne runs the perimeter. Thomas holds the suppression. The new ghost doesn't wait for orientation — he has three years of field intelligence and a shit list and unfinished business with every name on it. Above them, Bruce Wayne in the cave listening to Morrow's anniversary digging for the first time with his eyes open. Clark Kent in the city, within reach, for now. Alfred Pennyworth with medication on the nightstand and a very specific idea of where he wants to die. The Weight of Never Leaving is the third book of The House We Never Leave. It is the one where the fracture completes, the talon surfaces, a fourteen-year-old with a surveillance file and no survival instincts rings the doorbell at dusk, and Alfred Pennyworth dies in his chosen place. Clark was right. Bruce chooses the house anyway. He stands at the window. He does not run after him. Every heartbeat hurts.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Bat Princess and Cybertronian Bumblebee
entry.summary:
Author: NatalyaAglaevnaNaryshkina

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 54,498Chapters: 46/?Kudos: 5

Summary: [Transformers + DC, Crossover, Pairing: Bumblebee, Human/Transformer, Minor All Characters/OC, Heavy Headcanons]As the only daughter of a certain Dark Knight, the little princess of Gotham, Nova could get almost anything she wanted with ease. Money? Is there any entity richer than Wayne Enterprises? Strength? Come on, her father is Batman! For Miss Nova, there was no problem that money couldn't solve. If there was, it just meant she hadn't spent enough money—and she had more than enough of that. That was, until she bought a pair of broken glasses to help a classmate with his car-buying plan. Everything changed completely after that.Bumblebee's mission was to protect the holder of the glasses until his leader arrived on Earth. While he was waiting in the used car lot to be bought by Sam, that human girl named Nova smiled at him. From that moment on, that smile became a mark he could never erase from his processor.Nova had once asked him: "Bumblebee, do you like me?" Bumblebee did not answer, only feeling his spark beating abnormally fast. By the time he finally understood his own feelings, it was already too late. "I once had a star. Before she went out."

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: be a simple kind of man
entry.summary:
Author: outboard

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,896Chapters: 1/3Kudos: 38

Summary: On a stormy night in Gotham, Bruce's parents appear out of thin air, torn from the past. Between their sudden appearance, his children's insistence on meeting them, and having to explain the kind of man he's become, Bruce is not handling it well. On top of that, there is now a very real possibility that he'll have to come out to his parents. Bruce never imagined he'd ever get the chance—especially not in his mid-thirties—and now that he does, he's terrified."What happened?" he asks, smoothing a bandage over Bruce's stitches with careful, practiced movements. His hands are heavy. Warm. "What made you choose this?" And Bruce is—he's tired, and he's hurting, rubbed raw with simmering grief and overwhelm. His father stands over him, close enough to touch, caring for him in ways Bruce has only ever dreamt of for the past twenty-five years. Bruce sighs, quiet and aching and worn. "You died," he confesses. He'd never really been able to lie to his father.

entry.title: Worst First (Impression)
entry.summary:
Author: i8thecookie

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 35,237Chapters: 12/16Kudos: 192

Summary: Years ago, in Smallville, Bruce made an impression on Clark. And not a good one. Now Bruce is back in his life—charming, relentless, and completely unaware of the damage he left behind all those years ago. But Clark hasn't forgotten. And while Batman and Superman learn how to trust each other, their civilian identities are busy getting everything wrong.

entry.title: Man of Steel (-ing Your Date)
entry.summary:
Author: ripewallflower

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,675Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 13

Summary: Bruce is drunk and just wants to have a good time tonight. Clark isn't having it. Or After a lax night of drinking and playing the "clueless, playboy billionaire" at a Gotham gala, Bruce Wayne attempts to whisk away tonights lucky suitor to his private condo. However, his detective instincts are screaming that he’s being watched by something. A being that turns out to be a very impulsive, very dissatisfied Superman. The night takes a sharp turn when the Man of Steel decides to skip the formalities and literally pry open Bruce's car door in the middle of the street. Sounds fun, right?

entry.title: All The Things We Cannot See
entry.summary:
Author: Ivasa

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 20,609Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 69

Summary: People disappear in Gotham every day. Batman finds them. That’s the job. But information has a price, and sometimes it requires becoming someone else entirely. A different face, a different name, a different life - Bruce is good at it. To him, wearing a disguise is almost easier than being himself. This case should have been simple. It stops being simple the moment a rookie reporter from Metropolis decides that Matches Malone is exactly the kind of source he’s been looking for.

entry.title: The Things We Do for Love
entry.summary:
Author: RileyC

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,605Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 4

Summary: Clark had been assigned to do a story about the Miss Metropolis pageant, and while that held no interest for him he did uncover a string of mysterious deaths that piqued his curiosity. Needing some help from the World's Greatest Detective, he asked Bruce to lend a hand. In their civilian identities they conducted an investigation that led them to the culprit, while aggravating Metropolis P.D. detectives Maggie Sawyer and Dan Turpin. Detective Sawyer calling them the Hardy Boys sparked a plan in Bruce's head -- and that's the rest of the story.

entry.title: like grey skies (like a bird of paradise)
entry.summary:
Author: rainbow_nerds

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 53,669Chapters: 13/18Kudos: 444

Summary: He sighed to himself and downed the glass of champagne he'd been nursing—non-alcoholic, but enough to convince anyone who might give him a passing glance. He hated this, every time he had to do it. There was one surefire way to derail anyone who began wondering whether maybe Bruce Wayne was doing more than just donating funds to Batman. His tie hung loose around his neck, shirt unbuttoned to halfway down his chest, laughing too easily, pressing close to everyone he spoke to, whispering in ears, playing the part, until he turned and froze on the spot. Clark Kent, in his signature ill-fitting suit, stood holding a notebook and pen, staring at him with barely-concealed glee. Bruce cringed internally. He hated this. He especially hated when it had to be witnessed by someone he actually respected. or: After a Gala, Bruce and Clark end up in bed together. They're friends, just helping each other out, no strings attached. Which would be great, if Bruce hadn't been halfway to head-over-heels since the day they met.

entry.title: Once Upon a Time
entry.summary:
Author: WrenA

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,382Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 33

Summary: Once upon a time there was a prince. A lot of stories start with a prince or a princess. Or both. Or none. But they always tell of love. Bruce Wayne got shot. Very publicly so. In the wake of the tragedy, the bats close ranks, the JL realize they suddenly face a foe none of them had imagined and Gotham? Gotham despairs. ~~~~~~ An eldritch horror meets fairytales story.

entry.title: Simplest Words Make The Most Complicated Sentences
entry.summary:
Author: LuKuXing

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,274Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 79

Summary: In a world where your soulmate's first words were written on your body, Bruce couldn't think of anything other than how much he appreciated his soulmate, even if they weren't here. Soulmates were supposed to love you for who you were in your core. Would Bruce's soulmate love him as he is? Would they cherish him? Put up with him? Nevertheless, he was excited to meet his soulmate.

entry.title: The Long Way to Trust
entry.summary:
Author: serephent

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 31,484Chapters: 7/7Kudos: 13

Summary: Trust isn’t given. It’s built—one decision at a time. After the fall of Metropolis, the world is still standing. Barely. Bruce Wayne has seen what Superman can do—and what it costs. Clark Kent is still trying to understand what he’s become. The choices that brought them here aren’t finished with either of them. The next threat isn’t waiting. Working together wasn’t part of the plan. Trusting each other was never supposed to happen.

entry.title: When we meet again
entry.summary:
Author: misschini

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,926Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 40

Summary: Clark and Lois are getting a divorce and Jon has not taken it very well. Hoping some space will make him feel better, his father sends him to spend the whole summer at his "best friend's" house together with the youngest son of the family, Damian. Damian doesn't like that idea a lot.
